projects.question_en: Does a classifier trained to decode creative idea-generation trials on one creativity task (e.g., the Idea Diversity task or the Taxonomy of Verbal Creativity task) generalize to distinguishing hypothesis-generation from control trials on the Scientific Creativity Task (SCT), or is each task's neural signature domain-specific?
projects.question_es: ¿Un clasificador entrenado para decodificar ensayos de generación de ideas creativas en una tarea (p. ej. la tarea Idea Diversity o la Taxonomy of Verbal Creativity) generaliza a distinguir ensayos de generación de hipótesis vs. control en la Scientific Creativity Task (SCT), o la firma neural es específica de cada tarea?
projects.compute: University lab workstation or shared HPC; fMRIPrep preprocessing (~1-2 hrs/subject on 8 CPU cores), decoding analysis feasible on CPU only

required_variables.variable: task-SCT BOLD runs + events.tsv (hypothesis generation vs. matched control condition)
required_variables.note: 54 participants / 108 runs; non-empty event files for 54 participants and 107 runs; 53 participants have events for all SCT runs

required_variables.variable: task-ID BOLD runs + events.tsv (Idea Diversity task)
required_variables.note: 25 participants / 150 runs; non-empty event files for 19 participants and 114 runs

required_variables.variable: task-TVC BOLD runs + events.tsv (Taxonomy of Verbal Creativity task)
required_variables.note: 35 participants / 70 runs; non-empty event files for 35 participants and 70 runs
Defaced T1w anatomical scans included in the BIDS release

projects.still_open_because: The dataset's primary references (Beaty et al.; Luchini et al.; Zeitlen et al.) analyze each creativity task on its own; the evidence record does not describe any cross-task generalization/decoding analysis across SCT, ID, and TVC in this release.

projects.question_en: Can individual differences in resting-state functional connectivity (from the 108-subject resting-state scan) predict the magnitude or pattern of task-fMRI activation during hypothesis generation on the Scientific Creativity Task, i.e., does 'connectome fingerprinting' forecast task-evoked creative cognition?
projects.question_es: ¿Las diferencias individuales en conectividad funcional en reposo (de los 108 sujetos con resting-state) predicen la magnitud o el patrón de activación fMRI durante la generación de hipótesis en la Scientific Creativity Task, es decir, la 'huella conectómica' anticipa la cognición creativa evocada por la tarea?
projects.compute: Lab workstation or shared HPC; standard fMRIPrep preprocessing plus connectivity extraction, no GPU required

required_variables.note: 108 participants / 108 runs; events files not expected for rest

required_variables.variable: task-SCT BOLD + events.tsv (hypothesis generation vs. matched control condition)
required_variables.note: 54 participants / 108 runs; non-empty events for 54 participants and 107 runs

required_variables.variable: SBRef images matched to functional runs (for motion/coregistration QC)
required_variables.note: "Most functional runs include matched SBRef images"

required_variables.variable: Fieldmap EPI files linked to functional runs via BIDS metadata (for distortion correction)
required_variables.note: "Fieldmap EPI files are included where available and linked to the functional runs through BIDS metadata"

projects.still_open_because: The listed primary references study task-evoked and pattern-similarity effects of scientific/verbal creativity separately from resting state; the evidence does not indicate any rest-to-task predictive or connectome-fingerprinting analysis has been performed on this release.

projects.question_en: Using the matched hypothesis-generation-vs-control design built into the Scientific Creativity Task, what directed (effective-connectivity) influences among creativity-network regions distinguish hypothesis generation from the control condition, moving beyond simple activation contrasts toward a causal/generative model of scientific idea generation?
projects.question_es: Usando el diseño emparejado de generación de hipótesis vs. control incorporado en la Scientific Creativity Task, ¿qué influencias dirigidas (conectividad efectiva) entre regiones de la red de creatividad distinguen la generación de hipótesis de la condición control, yendo más allá de un simple contraste de activación hacia un modelo causal/generativo de la generación de ideas científicas?
skills: Dynamic Causal Modeling (SPM/MATLAB) or Granger causality (Python)
projects.compute: SPM/MATLAB or Python DCM/Granger toolboxes on a lab workstation; effective-connectivity estimation is CPU-bound with moderate per-subject runtime

required_variables.variable: task-SCT BOLD runs with hypothesis-generation and matched control condition events
required_variables.note: "task-SCT: Scientific Creativity Task, including hypothesis generation and a matched control condition"; 54 participants / 108 runs, events for 54 participants / 107 runs, 53 with complete events

required_variables.variable: Fieldmap EPI files linked to functional runs (for distortion-corrected timeseries needed by effective-connectivity models)

projects.still_open_because: Beaty et al. and related references report activation- or network-level contrasts for the control-vs-hypothesis-generation design; the evidence does not mention any directed/effective-connectivity (causal) modeling of that contrast having been performed.

projects.question_en: Can group-level task-fMRI activation during scientific hypothesis generation (task-SCT), combined with resting-state functional connectivity from the same 108 subjects, be used to derive a reproducible candidate neuromodulation target (e.g., in inferior frontal gyrus, implicated in creative category-switching per Luchini et al.) for future non-invasive stimulation studies aimed at enhancing creative ideation?
projects.question_es: ¿La activación fMRI a nivel grupal durante la generación de hipótesis científicas (task-SCT), combinada con la conectividad funcional en reposo de los mismos 108 sujetos, puede usarse para derivar un blanco candidato reproducible de neuromodulación (p. ej., en el giro frontal inferior, implicado en el cambio de categoría creativo según Luchini et al.) para futuros estudios de estimulación no invasiva orientados a potenciar la ideación creativa?
projects.compute: Lab workstation; group-level GLM and connectivity analysis, no specialized hardware needed

required_variables.variable: task-SCT BOLD + events.tsv (hypothesis generation vs. control) for group activation map
task-rest BOLD (108 subjects / 108 runs) for connectivity-based target refinement
T1w anatomical for individual/group registration and target coordinate mapping

projects.still_open_because: The IFG's role in creative category-switching is reported by Luchini et al. via a pattern-similarity analysis, not as a stimulation target; the evidence gives no indication this dataset has been used to derive or validate a connectivity-based neuromodulation target.

projects.question_en: Does self-supervised pretraining (e.g., masked-timepoint or contrastive representation learning) on the 108-subject/108-run resting-state fMRI corpus produce embeddings that improve few-shot classification of hypothesis-generation vs. control trials on task-SCT (labeled data available for only 54 participants), compared to training a classifier from scratch on the limited task data alone?
projects.question_es: ¿El preentrenamiento auto-supervisado (p. ej., aprendizaje de representaciones enmascaradas o contrastivas) sobre el corpus de fMRI en reposo de 108 sujetos/108 corridas produce representaciones (embeddings) que mejoran la clasificación few-shot de ensayos de generación de hipótesis vs. control en task-SCT (datos etiquetados disponibles solo para 54 participantes), comparado con entrenar un clasificador desde cero solo con los datos limitados de la tarea?
projects.compute: Single-GPU workstation (e.g., one RTX 3090/4090) sufficient for a 108-subject rs-fMRI pretraining corpus at this scale; a few days of training time

required_variables.variable: task-rest BOLD, full resting-state corpus for self-supervised pretraining
task-SCT BOLD + events.tsv (hypothesis generation vs. matched control) for downstream few-shot fine-tuning/evaluation
required_variables.note: labeled task data limited to 54 participants / 107 runs with non-empty events

required_variables.variable: T1w anatomical for standard-space registration ahead of representation learning

projects.still_open_because: None of the listed primary references describe a self-supervised/foundation-model pretraining approach on this release; all cited work uses conventional GLM, pattern-similarity, or network analyses on individual tasks.
